//: Copy this file to rooms/<game>/pxd/room.json and fill in your values.
Lines starting with '//' are comments and must be removed from your copy
Required fields are marked REQUIRED. All others have usable defaults.
//layout: Layout to use. 'default-dashboard' is the standard four-panel layout.
//title: REQUIRED — shown in the browser tab and page header
title: My Room Name
//topicRoot: REQUIRED — all default MQTT topics derive from this prefix
//mqtt: Connection settings. 'auto' is correct for nearly all deployments.

mqtt.//port: 'auto' = page port (80 for HTTP, 443 for HTTPS)
mqtt.//wsPath: Nginx WebSocket proxy path — matches the /mqtt location block
mqtt.//clientIdPrefix: Optional. Random suffix is appended automatically.

//theme: Visual theme. All fields are optional — omit any field to use the built-in
default. See docs/THEMING.md for the full token reference.

theme.//accent: Primary accent — buttons, focus rings, highlights
theme.//accentAlt: Secondary accent — success states, alternate highlights
theme.//fontBody: Body font stack. Must match a family name declared in 'fonts'.
theme.//fontMono: Monospace font stack (used for timers, codes, etc.)
//fonts: Declare @font-face entries for custom web fonts here.
'src' is relative to the packager output root (html/ directory).
Font files must be in rooms/<game>/pxd/fonts/ before packaging.
Remove this block if you are only using system fonts.

//media: Paths are relative to the packager output root.

media.//hero: Wide banner image shown at the top of the page. Landscape, ~1200x300 px.

//panels: Ordered list of panels to include. Determines load and mount order.
//gameControl: Game-control panel overrides. All fields are optional;
defaults derive from topicRoot. See docs/ROOMS.md for full reference.

//emergencyActions: Array of quick-action buttons shown in the emergency modal.

//timeLights: Time-lights panel overrides. All fields are optional.

timeLights.//lightsScenesTopicRoot: If set, subscribes to <value>/scenes for a dynamic scene list

//warningTopics: MQTT topics whose messages appear in the warning log.
//watchZones: One entry per service / device you want to monitor.
A zone goes to 'Disconnected' after timeoutMs ms without a message.
